setup_task.sh line 36: run('Fluorescent Cells (400K)'); confirmed via Fiji documentation search results
Data generated from Fiji's built-in sample image, not real experimental microscopy data

privileged_info_summary: This task uses synthetic data generated from Fiji's built-in Fluorescent Cells (400K) sample image. The raw data consists of three grayscale channels (mitochondria, cytoskeleton, nucleus) that must be assembled into a multi-channel composite TIFF with spatial calibration of 0.16 microns per pixel. The pixel width tolerance is ±0.01 microns (0.15-0.17 range). The expected unit for spatial calibration is 'micron'. Output must be saved to /home/ga/ImageJ_Data/processed/assembled_cell.tif as a multi-channel composite (not flattened RGB), with the green channel (cytoskeleton) adjusted for brightness/contrast visibility.
